commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8f76806b7c42417249a2339384ec867466faffb6)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Telegram polling restart/backoff policy, adds restart backoff state with stop-timeout cooldown and stop-call deduping, and adds focused polling-session tests.

PR surface: Source +81, Tests +116. Total +197 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: Source inspection provides a medium-confidence reproduction path: current main has the tight 2s/30s restart policy, and the default isolated path can return directly to another cycle after a forced stall restart. No live Telegram network-flap or stop-timeout reproduction was run.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor proof and maintainer review are still needed because automation cannot supply the required real Telegram/prod-like evidence for this external PR.

target.title: fix(telegram): slow polling restart storms
target.body:
## Summary
- Increase Telegram polling restart backoff from the tight 2s/30s policy to a bounded 30s/10m exponential policy.
- Deduplicate runner/bot stop calls per polling restart cycle so watchdog restarts do not stack repeated stop requests.
- Add stop-timeout burst cooldown and reset restart state after healthy normal and isolated-ingress polling cycles.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed:** Consecutive Telegram polling stop timeouts no longer restart on the same short 30s ceiling. The second stop-timeout burst applies a separate cooldown, while a healthy normal or isolated polling cycle resets restart attempts back to the first-delay path.
- **Real environment tested:** Local checkout `/Users/andy/openclaw` on branch `codex/telegram-poll-restart-backoff`, running the actual Telegram polling restart-delay helper and the actual `TelegramPollingSession` isolated-ingress restart loop with a fake Telegram worker/bot so no production token is required.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the helper proof below through `node --import tsx`, then ran a focused Vitest against `extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts` that starts an isolated-ingress session, lets one worker stop and restart, emits a real `poll-success` message from the next worker, and asserts the next restart backoff attempt resets from `[1, 2]` to `[1, 1]`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Helper output from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```text
firstStopTimeoutDelayMs=33000
firstStopTimeoutSuffix=""
secondStopTimeoutDelayMs=132000
secondStopTimeoutSuffix=" Stop timeout burst=2; applying cooldown."
afterHealthyDelayMs=33000
afterHealthySuffix=""
```

- **Production-like isolated polling proof:**

```text
$ node --no-maglev ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts -t "resets restart backoff after isolated ingress reports poll success" --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000 --hookTimeout=30000

 RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/andy/openclaw

-------*--------------------------------------------

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed | 51 skipped (52)
   Start at  17:38:32
   Duration  3.37s (transform 1.76s, setup 89ms, import 87ms, tests 3.10s, environment 0ms)
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The first stop timeout uses the normal 30s-class restart policy, the second consecutive stop timeout is raised by the stop-timeout cooldown path, the normal healthy cycle resets to the first-attempt delay, and the isolated-ingress `poll-success` path now also resets the real session backoff state before the next restart.
- **What was not tested:** No live Telegram network-flap run was performed. The full local `polling-session.test.ts` file also hits an existing `upstream/main` hang in `keeps active spooled lanes blocked across isolated ingress restarts`; this was reproduced in a detached `upstream/main` worktree, so verification used focused polling-session cases plus `monitor.test`.

## Verification
- `node --no-maglev ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts -t "drains Telegram delivery queue after isolated ingress reports poll success|resets restart backoff after isolated ingress reports poll success|uses backoff helpers|resets restart backoff|cools down repeated|forces a restart when polling stalls without getUpdates activity|forces a restart when the runner task is pending" --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000 --hookTimeout=30000`
- `node --no-maglev ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts -t "resets restart backoff after isolated ingress reports poll success" --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000 --hookTimeout=30000`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/monitor.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `git log --format='%h %an <%ae> %s' upstream/main..HEAD`

Refs #88256
target.author: turbotheturtle
